hooks.command: jq -r '.tool_input.command' | grep -qE '\| *(head|tail)\b' && echo 'Note: piping to head/tail can truncate exploration output (git log, grep, find). For status-checks (gh/git/jq one-liners) it is fine. For long output, prefer run_in_background + Read the output file.' >&2; exit 0
f="$HOME/.claude/hooks/no-emdash.py"; if [ -f "$f" ]; then python3 "$f"; else exit 0; fi

jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // .tool_response.filePath' | { read -r f; case "$f" in *.yml|*.yaml) output=$(yamllint -d relaxed --no-warnings "$f" 2>&1); rc=$?; if [ $rc -ne 0 ]; then printf '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PostToolUse","additionalContext":"YAML validation failed — fix these errors:\n%s"}}' "$output"; exit 1; fi ;; esac; }

jq -r '.tool_input.file_path' | { read -r f; case "$f" in $HOME/.claude/skills/*) skill_dir=$(echo "$f" | sed "s|$HOME/.claude/skills/||" | cut -d/ -f1); if [ -n "$skill_dir" ] && [ ! -L "$HOME/.claude/skills/$skill_dir" ]; then printf '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PostToolUse","additionalContext":"WARNING: Writing directly to ~/.claude/skills/%s/ — skills should live in ~/.agents/skills/ with a symlink. Create the skill in ~/.agents/skills/%s/ and symlink: ln -sf ../../.agents/skills/%s ~/.claude/skills/%s"}}' "$skill_dir" "$skill_dir" "$skill_dir" "$skill_dir"; exit 1; fi ;; esac; }

jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // .tool_response.filePath' | { read -r f; case "$f" in *.html) checker="$HOME/code/eagleeye/.agents/skills/html-output/scripts/check_self_contained.sh"; [ -x "$checker" ] || exit 0; v=$("$checker" "$f" 2>&1); rc=$?; if [ $rc -ne 0 ]; then printf '{"decision":"block","reason":"check_self_contained.sh failed for %s. Per /html-output, briefs must be single-file: via data: URIs, fonts via @font-face local()+Google Fonts fallback, no external

statusLine.command: input=$(cat); model=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.model.display_name'); cwd=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.workspace.current_dir'); dir=$(basename "$cwd"); usage=$($HOME/.claude/scripts/usage-cache.sh); h5=$(echo "$usage" | jq -r '.five_hour.utilization // "-"' | awk '{printf "%d", $0}'); d7=$(echo "$usage" | jq -r '.seven_day.utilization // "-"' | awk '{printf "%d", $0}'); echo "$model | $dir | 5h:${h5}% | 7d:${d7}%"
